rationale: The cited paper appears only in grouped citations to prior pharmaceutical primary-demand studies and to the broader market-expansion view of advertising. In the citing paper, these mentions serve background positioning (e.g., prior work finds small primary-demand effects) rather than assigning a distinct role to this specific study. There is no direct reuse of Fischer and Albers's method, decomposition, or empirical setup, and no focused comparison of results tied uniquely to that paper.
